note: The code is real: this is the ellipsis bug the Layer 5 reviewer flagged and the fixer repaired on 22 Jul 2026 (text[:length] + '...' -> text[:length], $0.000310, outcome 'fixed'). The ticket around it was written for this case, because the run's ticket was not captured. Runs-but-wrong in its purest form: it passes any test that only checks the text got shorter.

labels.text: the result never exceeds length characters
labels.why: the ellipsis is appended after the slice, so the result is length + 3 characters. truncate('hello world', 5) returns 'hello...', which is 8.

labels.text: a reader can tell that the text was shortened
labels.why: the trailing ellipsis is exactly that signal. This criterion is met; it is the reason the bug is tempting.
